SEARCH
Package neqsim.process.fielddevelopment.integrated

Interface ReservoirDrive

All Superinterfaces:
Serializable
All Known Implementing Classes:
AquiferDrive, MaterialBalanceGasDrive, OilTankDrive
public interface ReservoirDrive extends Serializable
A reservoir drive (material balance) model for integrated reservoir-to-market simulation.

A drive model translates cumulative produced volume into an average reservoir pressure, providing the coupling that lets the IntegratedProductionModel march a field forward in time. The pressure returned here is the boundary pressure applied to the reservoir nodes of the NetworkNewtonSolver, so well deliverability falls naturally as the reservoir depletes.

Version:
1.0
Author:
NeqSim
See Also:
  • MaterialBalanceGasDrive
  • AquiferDrive
  • OilTankDrive

  • Method Summary

    Modifier and Type
    Method
    Description
    double
    getCumulativeProduction()
    Returns the cumulative produced volume.
    double
    getInPlaceVolume()
    Returns the recoverable (or initial in-place) volume basis used by the drive.
    double
    getReservoirPressure()
    Returns the current average reservoir pressure.
    void
    produce(double producedVolumeSm3, double dtDays)
    Advances the drive by producing a volume over a time step.

  • Method Details

    • getReservoirPressure

      double getReservoirPressure()
      Returns the current average reservoir pressure.
      Returns:
      reservoir pressure in bara

    • produce

      void produce(double producedVolumeSm3, double dtDays)
      Advances the drive by producing a volume over a time step.
      Parameters:
      producedVolumeSm3 - surface volume produced during the step in Sm3
      dtDays - length of the time step in days

    • getCumulativeProduction

      double getCumulativeProduction()
      Returns the cumulative produced volume.
      Returns:
      cumulative production in Sm3

    • getInPlaceVolume

      double getInPlaceVolume()
      Returns the recoverable (or initial in-place) volume basis used by the drive.
      Returns:
      reference volume in Sm3
